noaa_coastwatch_erddap.name: NOAA CoastWatch ERDDAP — Great Lakes SST
noaa_coastwatch_erddap.description: GLSEA daily Great Lakes Surface Environmental Analysis: AVHRR+GOES composite SST at ~1.8 km. Covers all 5 Great Lakes. Best free thermal baseline before/after optical passes.
noaa_coastwatch_erddap.notes: Completely free, no registration. NetCDF download via ERDDAP subset URL. Use --sensors sst in universal_downloader.py

usgs_3dep_lidar.name: USGS 3D Elevation Program (3DEP) — LiDAR
usgs_3dep_lidar.description: National LiDAR point clouds and 1m/3m DEMs for all Great Lakes shorelines. Includes nearshore bathymetric LiDAR in some areas.

products.lpc: Lidar Point Cloud (.laz) — raw point returns

usgs_3dep_lidar.notes: Free, no registration. Use --sensors lidar. LPC files can be multi-GB — use --max-results 5 for initial test.

modis_thermal.name: MODIS MOD11A1/MYD11A1 — Daily 1km LST
modis_thermal.description: Terra and Aqua MODIS land surface temperature: 1-km daily, day+night passes. Best thermal complement to Landsat (much higher temporal frequency). Requires same Earthdata credentials as HLS/SWOT.
modis_thermal.notes: Use --sensors thermal or --sensors modis. Same EARTHDATA_TOKEN as HLS downloads.

icesat2_atl03.name: ICESat-2 ATL03 — Geolocated Photons (LiDAR)
icesat2_atl03.description: Individual photon returns from ICESat-2 ATLAS laser altimeter. ATL03 provides ~0.7m footprint at ~60-170m spacing. Water-penetrating green (532nm) channel enables shallow bathymetry in clear Great Lakes water to ~15-25m depth.
icesat2_atl03.notes: Same EARTHDATA_TOKEN. ATL03 files ~1-5 GB per granule. Use --sensors icesat2. Add --max-results 3 for test run.

usgs_earth_explorer.name: USGS Earth Explorer
usgs_earth_explorer.notes: Free registration. API key required for programmatic access.

copernicus_dataspace.name: Copernicus Data Space
copernicus_dataspace.description: Sentinel-1 SAR, Sentinel-2 optical. Primary source for SAR and high-res optical.
copernicus_dataspace.notes: Free registration. Username/password auth via OData API.

nasa_podaac.name: NASA PO.DAAC (SWOT, ICESat-2)
nasa_podaac.description: SWOT sea surface height, ICESat-2 ATL13 bathymetry. Ka-band radar altimetry.
nasa_podaac.notes: Free registration. Earthdata token required for downloads.

nasa_hls.name: NASA Harmonized Landsat Sentinel-2 (HLS)
nasa_hls.description: Combined Landsat-8/9 + Sentinel-2A/B surface reflectance. Seamless 30m optical.
nasa_hls.notes: Harmonized product — no need to process Landsat and Sentinel-2 separately. Earthdata token required.

sentinel_hub.name: Sentinel Hub (EO Browser / Process API)
sentinel_hub.description: Commercial-grade Sentinel processing with on-demand cloud processing.
sentinel_hub.notes: Free tier: 1000 processing units/month. Paid plans available.

noaa_class.name: NOAA Comprehensive Large Array-data Stewardship System (CLASS)
noaa_class.description: NOAA satellite data including GOERS, JPSS. Useful for thermal and atmospheric data.
noaa_class.notes: Free registration. Less commonly used for wreck detection but useful for atmospheric corrections.

asf_hyp3.name: ASF HyP3 (Hybrid Pluggable Processing Pipeline)
asf_hyp3.description: NASA Alaska Satellite Facility's cloud processing for SAR. Submit Sentinel-1 granules for on-demand RTC, InSAR, and autorift processing. Free — uses Earthdata auth.

processing_types.name: RTC
processing_types.description: Radiometric Terrain Corrected — calibrated backscatter (Gamma0). Best for wreck detection.

processing_types.name: InSAR
processing_types.description: Interferometric SAR — phase difference between two passes. Detects surface deformation.

processing_types.name: Autorift
processing_types.description: AutoRIFT — feature tracking for surface velocity / displacement fields.

rate_limits.queue_wait: Varies — typically 1-6 hours for RTC, longer for InSAR

best_for: RTC Gamma0 backscatter for submerged steel detection
InSAR coherence for hull mass verification
Free cloud processing — no local SNAP installation needed
asf_hyp3.notes: Uses same NASA Earthdata credentials as PO.DAAC. Free tier: 1 job at a time. Products delivered as GeoTIFF + metadata to S3 download link. Ideal for Straits of Mackinac where cloud cover ruins optical — SAR works in all weather.

straits_of_mackinac.reason: Ice cover typically melts off by late May. Best data is post-ice-melt through fall freeze-up.
straits_of_mackinac.cloud_cover_threshold: Less than 20% cloud coverage for optical
